fact: September 30 is exactly 273 days into the year - that's three-quarters of the way through! Only 92 days left until New Year's Eve.

In the Northern Hemisphere, leaves are going absolutely bonkers right now! Trees are pumping out crazy amounts of red and orange chemicals as they suck all the green chlorophyll back into their trunks.
Why do spiders suddenly appear everywhere around this time? Because it's spider dating season! Male spiders are frantically searching for mates before winter kills them.
The sun is setting almost exactly due west today in most places on Earth - no more of that weird summer sunset-in-the-northwest business.
Squirrels are basically going insane right now, burying up to 25 nuts per day and remembering where they put thousands of them using their supercharged autumn brains.
September 30 marks the end of meteorological autumn's first month - weather scientists split seasons differently than the calendar, and their autumn started September 1st.
In Antarctica, this is when the sun starts peeking over the horizon again after months of total darkness - like nature's most epic sunrise party!
Monarch butterflies are in the middle of their mind-blowing 3,000-mile journey south, and the ones flying today have never made this trip before but somehow know exactly where to go.
The Moon looks extra huge and orange when it rises tonight because of all the dust and pollen floating in the autumn air - it's like looking through a giant Instagram filter.
Deer are starting their weird autumn behavior called 'rutting' - male deer get so obsessed with showing off that they forget to eat and can lose 25% of their body weight.
September 30 is smack dab in the middle of harvest season, when plants are pumping their fruits and seeds full of sugar to make them irresistible to animals (including humans with apple cravings).

In northern countries, the aurora borealis starts getting more active around now because of the way Earth tilts toward the long polar night - it's like the sky's getting ready for its winter light show.
